'''WED-15-I662''', also known as '''Eye-Six-Six-Too''', was a [[WED-15 Treadwell droid/Legends|WED-15 Treadwell]] [[Maintenance droid/Legends|repair droid]] produced by the [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] company. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], I662 was aboard a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]], the [[Nebit's tribe/Legends|owners]] of which later captured the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Soon afterwards, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Desert stormtrooper/Legends|sandtroopers]] boarded the vehicle in search of the Rebel droids, but when the sandtroopers were unable to find them, they destroyed the sandcrawler.

==Biography==
WED-15-I662, also known as Eye-Six-Six-Too,<ref name="cswe" /> was a [[WED-15 Treadwell droid/Legends|WED-15 Treadwell]] [[Maintenance droid/Legends|repair droid]] manufactured by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]].<ref name="DB" /> sometime before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which includes WED-15-I662's onscreen presence, to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], I662 was aboard a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]]<ref name="ANH" /> operated by [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Nebit/Legends|Nebit's]] [[Nebit's tribe/Legends|clan]]<ref name="mhyd">{{DB|droid|mhyddroid|M-HYD droid}}</ref> when the [[Scavenger/Legends|scavengers]] captured the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and his [[Counterpart/Legends|counterpart]], the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. The next [[Standard day/Legends|day]], the sandcrawler stopped at the [[Lars homestead/Legends|homestead]] of [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]] [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]], where the Jawas wanted to sell some of their [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="ANH" /> During the auction, I662 was about to leave the droid [[Prison/Legends|prison]] of the crawler and join the other droids outside when a Jawa stopped it.<ref name="cross" />

The Jawas' auction was successful, as both C-3PO and R2-D2 were sold to Lars.<ref name="ANH" /> Soon afterward, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Desert stormtrooper/Legends|sandtroopers]] boarded the sandcrawler in search of the stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], which were stored in R2-D2. [[Davin Felth/Legends|Trooper 1023]] spotted I662 while he searched the droid bay for C-3PO and R2-D2. However, the sandtroopers were unable to find the plans, and the Imperials subsequently destroyed the sandcrawler.<ref name="desertwind">{{StoryCite|story=When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref>

==Characteristics==
WED-15-I662 was a standard [[WED Treadwell repair droid/Legends|Treadwell]], typical of the thousands of such droids tasked with maintenance and repair of heavy machinery and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="cswe" /> I662 had a [[Color/Legends|blue]] paint scheme and two rows of ten treaded wheels on<ref name="cross" /> its<ref name="cswe" /> base.<ref name="cross" /> The droid had [[Binoculars/Legends|binocular]] fine-focus vision, which helped it spot circuitry damage. I662's black [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] were mounted on a long, extensible neck strut,<ref name="vg7">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/explore/visualguides/chp07.html|text=Episode IV Visual Guide — Chapter 7: Land of the Jawas}}</ref> which also held its [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]].<ref name="DB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Treadwell-bts.jpg|thumb|250px|John Barry's production sketch of a Treadwell droid]]
In [[1975]], production designer [[John Barry]] designed the WED-15 unit model for use in [[George Lucas]]'s [[1977]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="making">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref> The model was used for WED-15-I662 as well as the droid [[WED-15-77/Legends|WED-15-77]] and [[Unidentified WED-15 Treadwell (Lars homestead)|another Treadwell]] during the filming of the movie.<ref name="DB" /> Barry's sketch indicated that the model stood between four feet and four feet, six inches tall. Barry's notes showed that the prop had one functioning arm and housed a radio controller in its treadwell base to allow for off-screen crew members to control it. Scenes featuring the droid were shot on location in [[Tunisia]] in [[March]] [[1976]].<ref name="making" />

I662 first appeared in the [[Star Wars (1977) 1|first issue]] of [[Marvel Comics]]' adaptation of the film, inside the Jawas' sandcrawler.<ref name="marvel">[[Star Wars (1977) 1|''Star Wars'' (1977) 1]]</ref> The comic was published on [[April 12]], 1977,<ref name="Marvel April">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53791/star_wars_1977_1|text=Star Wars (1977) #1}} {{C|First printing}} {{C|The date on the Marvel.com live page is the cover date, [[July 1]], but it was previously [[April 12]], the on-sale date. This information can still be accessed on the provided backup link.}}</ref> prior to I662's live action appearance in the film itself.<ref name="ANH" /> The droid went unnamed until [[1995]], when the [[Premiere Limited|base set]] of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] identified it as "WED15-I662."<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=Premiere Limited|cardname=WED15-I662 "Treadwell" Droid}}</ref> Later, in [[2008]], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' added a second hyphen to the droid's designation, thus making it similar to the names of other Treadwell droids.<ref name="cswe" />
